summary: Justin Brennan teaches a comprehensive 90-day framework for buying multifamily apartments (15-50 units), focusing on three critical pillars: finding deals through broker relationships, analyzing deals using specific financial metrics (14-20% annual returns, 6-10% cash-on-cash, 1.8x equity multiple), and raising capital through syndication. The methodology emphasizes targeting pre-market deals, understanding net operating income (NOI), and structuring deals with general/limited partnerships to create multiple revenue streams.
takeaways: Target 10-30 unit deals for first properties; brokers will respond and lenders will finance easier than 50+ unit deals
For every $1,000 monthly NOI increase, add $220,000 in property value
Aim for 14-20% annual returns, 6-10% cash-on-cash return, and 1.8x equity multiple minimum on deals
Contact 'mom and pop' brokers directly: 'Hey Austin, I'm looking at your 23-unit on Melrose Court, been on market 51 days, how's activity?'
60% of deals sell pre-market before hitting websites; only overpriced deals appear online
General partner structure: bring 10% of money, get 30% of deal (acquisition fee 2-3%, asset management fee 1-2%, cash flow/profit splits, disposition fee)
Use fixed-rate debt (3-5-7-10 year terms, amortized over 30 years); never over-leverage; prioritize properties purchased 2021-2022 as distressed opportunities

skill_candidates.change: Learn to identify deals meeting three core metrics instead of analyzing every online listing
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin explains that 60% of good deals sell pre-market; only 'overpriced deals' appear on CoStar/LoopNet. He teaches: 14-20% annual IRR, 6-10% cash-on-cash, 1.8x equity multiple. For the 23-unit example, he shows how NOI of $1,000/month = $220,000 in value, making this the key analysis metric.

skill_candidates.change: Build systematic broker relationships by touring properties and establishing rapport to access pre-market deals
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin demonstrates exact cold call script: 'Hey Austin, I'm looking at your 23-unit deal on Melrose Court, been on market 51 days, how's activity?' He explains brokers will call you back on pre-market deals if you show genuine interest in on-market listings first. Start with 10-30 unit range where 'mom and pop brokers will call you back.'

skill_candidates.change: Execute deal analysis and capital raising in 2-3 hour nightly windows while employed; commit to 90-day timeline despite full-time job
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin shares his own timeline: worked 10 hours/day in luxury real estate sales, then 8:30pm-11pm on deal sourcing/analysis for 2+ years before first Kansas City deal in 2018-2019. He emphasizes 'walk before you run' and starting with smaller deals (10-30 units) to learn the process.

title: How To Buy Multifamily Apartments In 90 Days – Real Estate Investing Guide
